title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present a system for converting a large personal knowledge vault into actionable context for AI agents. The approach uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, indexed layer, wiki layer) to efficiently organize thousands of notes and documents, enabling deep research queries across personal and web sources without requiring vector or graph databases.
writeup: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard demonstrate a system for turning a large personal knowledge vault (10,994 notes and documents) into actionable context for AI agents. The architecture uses three layers: raw content, an indexed layer, and a wiki layer, organizing thousands of notes without relying on vector or graph databases. This setup supports deep research queries that span both personal notes and web sources, addressing how to make accumulated knowledge usable as agent memory.
